Package org.areslib.core

Class StateMachine<S extends Enum<S>>

java.lang.Object
org.areslib.core.StateMachine<S>
Type Parameters:
S - The enum type representing the states.
public class StateMachine<S extends Enum<S>> extends Object
A lightweight, enum-based finite state machine for managing subsystem complexity.

Provides entry/exit/during actions per state, conditional and timeout transitions, validated transition tables, and automatic telemetry logging. State changes and illegal transition attempts are both logged for debugging.

Validated Transition Mode: When transitions are explicitly registered via addTransition(S, S), addValidBidirectional(S, S), addWildcardTo(S), or addWildcardFrom(S), the machine operates in validated mode. In this mode, any transition not explicitly whitelisted is rejected and logged as an error. If no transitions are explicitly registered, all transitions are allowed (legacy behavior). 


 enum IntakeState { IDLE, EXTENDING, GRIPPING, RETRACTING, STOWED }

 StateMachine<IntakeState> sm = new StateMachine<>("Intake", IntakeState.class, IntakeState.IDLE);
 sm.addTransition(IDLE, EXTENDING);
 sm.addTransition(EXTENDING, GRIPPING);
 sm.addValidBidirectional(GRIPPING, RETRACTING);
 sm.addWildcardTo(IDLE); // any state can abort to IDLE

 sm.onEntry(EXTENDING, () -> slides.setPower(1.0));
 sm.during(GRIPPING, () -> gripper.holdPosition());
 sm.transition(EXTENDING, GRIPPING, () -> slides.atTarget());
 sm.transitionAfter(GRIPPING, RETRACTING, 0.5); // 500ms timeout

 // In periodic():
 sm.update();

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    StateMachine(String name, Class<S> enumClass, S initialState)
    Constructs a StateMachine with a name, enum class, and initial state.
    StateMachine(String name, Class<S> enumClass, S initialState, DoubleSupplier timeSource)
    Constructs a StateMachine with an explicit time source for deterministic testing.
    StateMachine(S initialState)
    Constructs a StateMachine with an auto-derived name and initial state.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addTransition(S from, S to)
    Registers a one-directional transition from from to to.
    void
    addValidBidirectional(S a, S b)
    Registers transitions in both directions between a and b.
    void
    addWildcardFrom(S fromState)
    Makes fromState able to transition to every state in the enum.
    void
    addWildcardTo(S targetState)
    Makes targetState reachable from every state in the enum.
    void
    during(S state, Runnable action)
    Registers an action to run every loop tick while in a state.
    void
    emitTransitionGraph()
    Emits the validated transition table as a DOT-format graph string to telemetry.
    void
    forceState(S newState)
    Forces a state change, bypassing the validated transition table.
    String
    getName()
    Returns the human-readable name of this state machine.
    S
    getPreviousState()
    Returns the previous state.
    S
    getState()
    Returns the current state.
    double
    getTimeInState()
    Returns the time (in seconds) spent in the current state.
    int
    getTotalTransitionCount()
    Returns the total number of accepted (non-self) transitions since construction.
    Map<S,EnumSet<S>>
    getValidTransitions()
    Returns the validated transition table as an unmodifiable map, or null if in unvalidated mode.
    boolean
    isInState(S state)
    Returns true if the machine is in the given state.
    boolean
    isTransitionLegal(S nextState)
    Checks whether a transition from the current state to nextState would be accepted.
    void
    onEntry(S state, Runnable action)
    Registers an action to run once when entering a state.
    void
    onExit(S state, Runnable action)
    Registers an action to run once when exiting a state.
    boolean
    requestTransition(S newState)
    Requests a state change.
    void
    setOnTransition(BiConsumer<S,S> callback)
    Registers a callback that fires on every accepted transition, receiving the source and destination states.
    void
    transition(S from, S to, BooleanSupplier condition)
    Registers a conditional transition from one state to another.
    void
    transitionAfter(S from, S to, double timeoutSeconds)
    Registers a timeout-based transition that fires after spending a given duration in the source state.
    void
    update()
    Evaluates transitions and executes state actions.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • StateMachine

      public StateMachine(String name, Class<S> enumClass, S initialState)
      Constructs a StateMachine with a name, enum class, and initial state.

      The name is used in telemetry log keys for identifying which state machine produced a log entry. The enum class is required for EnumSet construction in the validated transition table.

      Parameters:
      name - Human-readable name for log output (e.g., "Intake", "Elevator").
      enumClass - The enum class token (e.g., IntakeState.class).
      initialState - The state the machine starts in.

    • StateMachine

      public StateMachine(String name, Class<S> enumClass, S initialState, DoubleSupplier timeSource)
      Constructs a StateMachine with an explicit time source for deterministic testing.
      Parameters:
      name - Human-readable name for log output.
      enumClass - The enum class token.
      initialState - The state the machine starts in.
      timeSource - A supplier returning the current time in seconds.

    • StateMachine

      public StateMachine(S initialState)
      Constructs a StateMachine with an auto-derived name and initial state.

      The name is derived from the enum class simple name. This constructor is kept for backwards compatibility.

      Parameters:
      initialState - The state the machine starts in.

  • Method Details

    • addTransition

      public void addTransition(S from, S to)
      Registers a one-directional transition from from to to.

      Once any transition is explicitly registered, the machine enters validated mode: only whitelisted transitions are allowed; all others are rejected and logged.

      Parameters:
      from - The source state.
      to - The destination state.

    • addValidBidirectional

      public void addValidBidirectional(S a, S b)
      Registers transitions in both directions between a and b.
      Parameters:
      a - The first state.
      b - The second state.

    • addWildcardTo

      public void addWildcardTo(S targetState)
      Makes targetState reachable from every state in the enum.
      Parameters:
      targetState - The state that all others can transition to.

    • addWildcardFrom

      public void addWildcardFrom(S fromState)
      Makes fromState able to transition to every state in the enum.
      Parameters:
      fromState - The state that can go anywhere.

    • isTransitionLegal

      public boolean isTransitionLegal(S nextState)
      Checks whether a transition from the current state to nextState would be accepted.
      Parameters:
      nextState - The proposed destination state.
      Returns:
      true if the transition is legal (including self-transitions).

    • onEntry

      public void onEntry(S state, Runnable action)
      Registers an action to run once when entering a state.
      Parameters:
      state - The state.
      action - The action.

    • onExit

      public void onExit(S state, Runnable action)
      Registers an action to run once when exiting a state.
      Parameters:
      state - The state.
      action - The action.

    • during

      public void during(S state, Runnable action)
      Registers an action to run every loop tick while in a state.
      Parameters:
      state - The state.
      action - The action.

    • transition

      public void transition(S from, S to, BooleanSupplier condition)
      Registers a conditional transition from one state to another.
      Parameters:
      from - The source state.
      to - The target state.
      condition - The condition that triggers the transition.

    • transitionAfter

      public void transitionAfter(S from, S to, double timeoutSeconds)
      Registers a timeout-based transition that fires after spending a given duration in the source state.
      Parameters:
      from - The source state.
      to - The target state.
      timeoutSeconds - The time in seconds before auto-transitioning.

    • setOnTransition

      public void setOnTransition(BiConsumer<S,S> callback)
      Registers a callback that fires on every accepted transition, receiving the source and destination states.
      Parameters:
      callback - A BiConsumer that receives (fromState, toState).

    • update

      public void update()
      Evaluates transitions and executes state actions. Must be called every loop iteration.

    • requestTransition

      public boolean requestTransition(S newState)
      Requests a state change. If validated transitions are configured and the transition is illegal, the request is rejected and an error is logged.
      Parameters:
      newState - The desired state.
      Returns:
      true if the transition was accepted, false if rejected.

    • forceState

      public void forceState(S newState)
      Forces a state change, bypassing the validated transition table. Use with caution — this should only be called for emergency resets or initialization.
      Parameters:
      newState - The state to transition to.

    • getState

      public S getState()
      Returns the current state.
      Returns:
      The current state.

    • getPreviousState

      public S getPreviousState()
      Returns the previous state.
      Returns:
      The previous state.

    • isInState

      public boolean isInState(S state)
      Returns true if the machine is in the given state.
      Parameters:
      state - The state to check.
      Returns:
      True if in the given state.

    • getTimeInState

      public double getTimeInState()
      Returns the time (in seconds) spent in the current state.
      Returns:
      Time in current state.

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns the human-readable name of this state machine.
      Returns:
      The machine name.

    • getTotalTransitionCount

      public int getTotalTransitionCount()
      Returns the total number of accepted (non-self) transitions since construction.
      Returns:
      Cumulative transition count.

    • emitTransitionGraph

      public void emitTransitionGraph()
      Emits the validated transition table as a DOT-format graph string to telemetry. Only meaningful if transitions have been explicitly registered via addTransition(S, S).

      Call this once after all transitions are registered (e.g., at the end of subsystem construction). The output is logged under StateMachine/<name>/Graph.

    • getValidTransitions

      public Map<S,EnumSet<S>> getValidTransitions()
      Returns the validated transition table as an unmodifiable map, or null if in unvalidated mode.
      Returns:
      The transition table, or null.
